metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py (VerifySignatureResultOut) and src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json as edit targets if the rebar_version stamp is formally part of the verify_signature return contract.
findings.finding: AC6 and the 'returned by verify_signature' claim are unenumerated consumers of the stamp: the MCP wrapper src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py validates the verify_signature result through VerifySignatureResultOut (a Pydantic model without a rebar_version field), and src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json is the typed contract. If the stamp is formally a new key on the returned dict, both are unenumerated edit targets.
findings.location: AC6 / src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py:243 / src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC6 the specific field name (e.g. `rebar_version`), its type (`str | None`), and explicitly require updating `verify_signature_result.schema.json` and `VerifySignatureResultOut` to include it.
findings.finding: AC6 says the `rebar-version` stamp is 'returned by `verify_signature`', but neither the `verify_signature_result.schema.json` schema (which has `required` fields and `additionalProperties: true`) nor the `VerifySignatureResultOut` Pydantic model (which mirrors that schema) is mentioned as needing extension. The plan never specifies the field name, type, or nullability for this new return key, leaving the output contract undefined for an executor.
findings.location: AC6 / src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json / src/rebar/_mcp_models.py VerifySignatureResultOut
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'The `verify_signature` return dict gains a `rebar_version: str | None` key (populated by calling `manifest_rebar_version()` on the result manifest); update `verify_signature_result.schema.json` and `VerifySignatureResultOut` accordingly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan: (1) the specific `.gitignore` file that must receive `_build_info.py`, (2) an `artifacts` entry in `pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]` to force-include the generated file, and (3) the `[tool.hatch.build.hooks]` pyproject.toml stanza needed to register the build hook.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `src/rebar/_build_info.py` will be 'git-ignored', but there is no existing `.gitignore` entry for it and no `artifacts:` entry in pyproject.toml to force-include it in the wheel. Both gaps must be addressed for the baked-fallback path to work on PyPI installs.
findings.location: AC #3: 'A hatchling build hook (BuildHookInterface) captures the commit SHA at wheel/sdist build into a generated, git-ignored src/rebar/_build_info.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to pyproject.toml `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] artifacts`: `"src/rebar/_build_info.py"`, add `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` with `path = "hatch_build.py"`, and add `src/rebar/_build_info.py` to the root `.gitignore`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `hatch_build.py` file path (repo root) and a `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` stanza to `pyproject.toml` as explicit edit targets in the plan.
findings.finding: The hatchling build hook (AC3) requires registering a [tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom] stanza in pyproject.toml AND placing hatch_build.py at the repo root (hatchling's discovery convention), but the plan names neither the hook file's path nor the required pyproject.toml stanza. Without both, hatchling silently skips the hook at wheel/sdist build time.
findings.location: AC3 / pyproject.toml
findings.suggested_fix: Add to pyproject.toml: `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` (no path key needed — hatchling discovers `hatch_build.py` at the repo root by convention). Name `hatch_build.py` at the repo root as the new file implementing `BuildHookInterface`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the required `pyproject.toml` registration for the hatchling build hook (e.g. a `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` stanza specifying the hook file path) alongside the `BuildHookInterface` implementation, so hatchling actually invokes it during `pip wheel` / `pip install` builds.
findings.finding: The plan specifies implementing a `BuildHookInterface` hatchling build hook to bake the commit SHA into `src/rebar/_build_info.py` at wheel/sdist build time, but does not specify the required `pyproject.toml` registration (`[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` stanza and hook file location). Without this declaration, hatchling silently ignores the hook — no `_build_info.py` is ever baked into PyPI wheels, making the entire 'baked-second' fallback path dead code at the exact install scenario it is designed for.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC #3 ('A hatchling build hook (`BuildHookInterface`) captures the commit SHA…')
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to pyproject.toml:
```toml
[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]
path = "hatch_build.py"
```
and document that `hatch_build.py` at the repo root is where the `BuildHookInterface` subclass lives. Alternatively, package it as a named hook under `src/rebar/_build_hook.py` with the appropriate `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.rebar-sha]` declaration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan: (a) the `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` pyproject.toml stanza (with hook file path) that registers the `BuildHookInterface` hook, and (b) an `artifacts` entry for `src/rebar/_build_info.py` under `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]` so the git-ignored generated file is actually included in the wheel — mirroring the existing `src/rebar/_engine/**/*` precedent.
findings.finding: The hatchling BuildHookInterface integration is missing two required registration artefacts: (1) the pyproject.toml `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]` stanza that activates the hook, and (2) an `artifacts` glob for the generated `src/rebar/_build_info.py` in `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]`. Without the `artifacts` entry, hatchling's git-based source-file discovery silently drops the git-ignored generated file from the wheel — the baked-SHA fallback never reaches PyPI installs, defeating the entire purpose of the hook.
findings.location: AC #3 ('A hatchling build hook (BuildHookInterface) captures the commit SHA at wheel/sdist build into a generated, git-ignored src/rebar/_build_info.py') and pyproject.toml §[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
findings.suggested_fix:
In pyproject.toml, add:
```toml
[tool.hatch.build.hooks.custom]
path = "hatch_build.py"
```
and extend the `artifacts` list under `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]`:
```toml
artifacts = [
    "src/rebar/_engine/**/*",
    "src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/dist/**/*",
    "src/rebar/_build_info.py",   # generated by hatch_build.py; git-ignored
]
```
Then name `hatch_build.py` (at repo root) as the hook file location in the plan's scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Route the SHA-resolution helper's live-git calls (`rev-parse --short HEAD`, `status --porcelain`) through `run_git()` from `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` (with `check=False`) rather than inline `subprocess.run`, consistent with the codebase's established git-subprocess consolidation.
findings.finding: The SHA-resolution helper is planned to call `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> rev-parse --short HEAD` directly (and `git status --porcelain`) via an inline subprocess invocation, but `run_git()` in `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` is the codebase's established, consolidated git-subprocess wrapper used at 10+ call-sites. Bypassing it for the live-git path creates a parallel git-invocation pattern, exactly the drift `run_git()` was introduced to prevent.
findings.location: Plan section 'What gets recorded' / SHA-resolution helper, step 1; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Import `run_git` from `rebar._store.gitutil` in the helper and call `run_git(rebar_src_dir, 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD', check=False)` / `run_git(rebar_src_dir, 'status', '--porcelain', check=False)` in place of bare `subprocess.run`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan-body section (e.g., 'Reading / display path') that describes (a) the `manifest_rebar_version()` parser contract, (b) exactly which fields `verify_signature` adds to its return value, and (c) what the `show` attestation block renders for the new stamp — mirroring the detail already given for the write path.
findings.finding: AC4 (manifest_rebar_version() parser) and AC6 (stamp surfaced in 'show' and returned by 'verify_signature') describe concrete deliverables — a parsing function and changes to two display/verification call-sites — that have no corresponding description anywhere in the plan body. An implementer reading the plan body would have no guidance on what 'show' displays, how 'verify_signature' is modified, or what the parser's contract is beyond the AC text alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria lines 4 and 6 vs. plan body sections ('What gets recorded', 'Context', 'Success Criteria')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Reading / display path' subsection under 'What gets recorded' or as a sibling section, covering: the parser signature (`manifest_rebar_version(text: str) -> str | None`), the key it seeks (`rebar-version:`), the `None`-on-absence contract, the new field name added to `verify_signature`'s return dict, and the label used in the `show` attestation block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Context section to state that `_verdict_manifest` lives in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`, not in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, and update AC #1 to name both files explicitly.
findings.finding: _verdict_manifest is asserted to live in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py, but it actually lives in src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py. This mislocation could cause implementors to modify the wrong file or miss the correct one.
findings.location: Context section: 'build_manifest (plan-review) and _verdict_manifest (completion-verifier) in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Context: 'Signed manifests are built by `build_manifest` (plan-review) in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` and `_verdict_manifest` (completion-verifier) in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test scenarios (or proving commands) for `show` displaying the `rebar-version:` line in the attestation block and for `verify_signature` returning the stamp, so AC #6 has concrete, in-session evidence of completion.
findings.finding: AC #6 ('The stamp is surfaced in `show` (attestation block) and returned by `verify_signature`') has no corresponding test or proving command in the test list, leaving it unmeasured.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 6: 'The stamp is surfaced in `show` (attestation block) and returned by `verify_signature`.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add two test cases to the test list: (1) a `show` output fixture test asserting the attestation block contains `rebar-version: ...`; (2) a `verify_signature` return-value assertion confirming the stamp is present in the returned dict/namedtuple.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command (e.g. `python -m build --wheel` in CI or a subprocess-based integration test) that verifies the hatchling build hook actually generates `_build_info.py` with a commit SHA during a real sdist/wheel build.
findings.finding: The hatchling build hook AC ('A hatchling build hook captures the commit SHA at wheel/sdist build into `src/rebar/_build_info.py`') has no proving command that exercises the hook executing during an actual wheel build, only indirect coverage via the 'baked fallback' read test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 3: 'A hatchling build hook (`BuildHookInterface`) captures the commit SHA at wheel/sdist build into a generated, git-ignored `src/rebar/_build_info.py`; absent-git build omits it gracefully.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a log/warning (at debug or warning level) whenever SHA resolution falls back to the baked value or omits the SHA entirely, so operators can distinguish expected (PyPI install) from unexpected (git present but failing) cases.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify any observability (log/warning/debug output) when SHA resolution falls back from live-checkout to baked or to omit. An operator or developer inspecting a manifest with no SHA cannot tell whether the gate code was a PyPI wheel (expected) or whether a live checkout silently failed SHA resolution.
findings.location: 'What gets recorded' section — SHA resolution fallback; AC line for SHA-resolution helper
findings.suggested_fix: Emit `logging.debug('rebar: live git SHA unavailable, falling back to baked SHA')` or `logging.warning('rebar: SHA resolution failed, omitting from manifest')` at each fallback step in the helper.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the Tests AC explicit coverage for the `show` and `verify_signature` surfacing paths: e.g. 'show output includes the rebar-version line when stamp is present; verify_signature return value includes the stamp field; both degrade gracefully (no crash, field absent/None) on pre-stamp manifests.'
findings.finding: The acceptance criteria require that the stamp is 'surfaced in `show` (attestation block) and returned by `verify_signature`', but the Tests AC does not include any test work for these two surfacing paths.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet ('The stamp is surfaced in show...') vs. Tests bullet

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC2 the exact scope and interpretation for dirty detection: which directory is passed to `git -C`, and whether untracked files (`??` lines) count as dirty.
findings.finding: The `-dirty` marker decision rule is under-specified: the plan states 'git status --porcelain' but does not define what output counts as dirty (untracked-only? staged? modified-tracked? all of the above?) nor whether the scope is the rebar source directory (`git -C <rebar_dir>`) or the full working tree root. This is a processing-rule gap co-located with the live-checkout path.
findings.location: Plan section 'What gets recorded' / `-dirty` marker bullet / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Dirty detection runs `git -C <dir-of-rebar.__file__> status --porcelain`; any non-empty output line (including `??` untracked) marks the tree dirty — matching the broad definition an auditor expects.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.gitignore` as an explicit edit target, with the entry `src/rebar/_build_info.py` to honour the plan's 'git-ignored' guarantee.
findings.finding: The plan states src/rebar/_build_info.py will be 'git-ignored' but does not list .gitignore as an edit target. The current .gitignore has no entry for this file, so without an explicit addition the generated file will appear as an untracked change in dev checkouts that run a build.
findings.location: Context / What gets recorded / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Append `src/rebar/_build_info.py` to .gitignore.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `show` display for pre-stamp manifests where `manifest_rebar_version()` returns `None` (e.g., omit the line entirely, or display `rebar-version: (not recorded)`).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how `show` (attestation block) renders the `rebar-version` field when `manifest_rebar_version()` returns `None` for pre-stamp manifests.
findings.location: AC: 'The stamp is surfaced in `show` (attestation block)'
findings.suggested_fix: Omit the `rebar-version:` line from `show` output when the value is `None`, to avoid displaying a confusing empty or null field for legacy attestations.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file/function responsible for 'show (attestation block)' rendering in AC6 — e.g. whether it is the verify_signature_cli text output (signing.py), a reads_cli.py show arm, or a separate display helper.
findings.finding: AC6 says the stamp is 'surfaced in `show` (attestation block)' but no specific file or function that renders the attestation block in show output is named. There is no attestation-specific show command in src/rebar/_commands/; the attestation data lives inside the ticket state dict returned by show_ticket/show_state. The plan leaves it ambiguous which rendering layer (CLI, library return, MCP tool output) needs updating.
findings.location: AC6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific function/module that renders the attestation block in `show` (e.g. the relevant `_commands/` file or CLI output path) and name it explicitly in AC6.
findings.finding: AC6 says the stamp is 'surfaced in `show` (attestation block)' but the plan never identifies which command, function, or module implements `show` for attestations in the existing codebase. No `show_attestation` function exists; the attestation display path is not identified. An executor cannot know where to add the surfacing code.
findings.location: AC6 / plan section 'Acceptance Criteria'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the SHA-resolution helper wraps ALL subprocess invocations in a broad exception handler (catching non-zero exit codes AND OS-level exceptions), treating any failure — not just 'git not found' — as the 'omit SHA' fallback case.
findings.finding: The SHA-resolution helper's error handling only specifies the 'git not installed/not a checkout' fallback path; it does not specify what happens when git IS present but the subprocess fails with a non-zero exit code or raises an OS-level exception (e.g., PermissionError, timeout, corrupted repo). An unhandled exception here could break manifest signing entirely.
findings.location: 'What gets recorded' section — SHA resolution steps 1 and 2; AC: 'A SHA-resolution helper implements live-git-first, build-baked-second, omit-last'
findings.suggested_fix: In the helper, use a single `try/except Exception` (or `subprocess.CalledProcessError` + `OSError` + `FileNotFoundError`) around both `rev-parse` and `git status` calls, logging a debug/warning on unexpected failures and falling through to the baked or omit path. The build hook should similarly catch all git errors, not only the absent-git case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC2 (SHA-resolution helper) to specify that ANY subprocess failure (non-zero exit, OSError, TimeoutExpired) on the live-git path degrades to the baked fallback, mirroring the `signing.head_sha()` pattern.
findings.finding: The SHA-resolution helper's fallback protocol only specifies the 'git not a checkout / not installed' case, but does not specify what happens when git IS present yet the subprocess fails non-fatally (non-zero exit code, `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`, or permission error on `rev-parse --short HEAD`). The existing `signing.head_sha()` uses `check=False` + returncode inspection + `OSError` catch; the plan is silent on which exception classes and exit-code conditions trigger the 'omit SHA' fallback vs. re-raise.
findings.location: Plan section 'What gets recorded' / SHA-resolution step 1 / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Any failure of the live-git subprocess (OSError, non-zero returncode, TimeoutExpired) falls through to the baked path, not to omit; omit is only the final tier when both live-git and baked are unavailable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `rebar.__version__` is dynamically resolved via `importlib.metadata` (not a compile-time constant), note the '0+unknown' fallback for un-installed checkouts, and confirm the SHA-resolution helper AC still provides meaningful provenance in that case.
findings.finding: The plan calls rebar.__version__ a 'static package version', but the actual implementation resolves it dynamically at import time via importlib.metadata.version('nava-rebar'), falling back to '0+unknown' in un-installed dev checkouts. This means on a plain source-tree checkout without an editable install, rebar.__version__ would be '0+unknown' rather than '0.6.0'.
findings.location: What gets recorded: '**Version**: `rebar.__version__` (static package version)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief note to CLAUDE.md (or an ADR) documenting the two-phase SHA resolution design decision — live-checkout wins over baked, baked is for PyPI installs, `_build_info.py` is generated and git-ignored — so future contributors understand the pattern.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel two-phase SHA resolution pattern (live-git-first at runtime + build-time bake into `_build_info.py`) that crosses the build system boundary, but does not mention capturing this architectural decision in a persistent project document (CLAUDE.md, ADR, or design doc).
findings.location: 'What gets recorded' section; AC: hatchling build hook; Scope/non-goals

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `os.path.dirname(rebar.__file__)` resolves to the source-tree root (not a site-packages shim) under hatchling's editable install (`pip install -e .`), and document the fallback or use an alternative anchor (e.g., `importlib.resources` files path, `__file__` of a known source-tree module, or an explicit `REBAR_SOURCE_ROOT` env var).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the live-git path resolves the rebar source checkout via 'git -C <dir of rebar.__file__>', and that this reliably hits the editable/dev case. Under hatchling's editable install mechanism (which uses an import hook or .pth file rather than symlinking), rebar.__file__ may point into site-packages rather than the source tree, causing the live-git path to silently fall through to the baked value even in a dev checkout — defeating the stated 'wins over any baked value' guarantee.
findings.location: 'What gets recorded' section, bullet 1: 'live git checkout of the rebar source -> git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> rev-parse --short HEAD (dev/editable: the live truth, wins over any baked value)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate whether `hatch-vcs`'s `write` feature (which generates a metadata file at build time with commit SHA, independent of its version-string management) covers AC #3 before committing to a custom `BuildHookInterface`; if not, document why.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a custom hatchling BuildHookInterface to bake the commit SHA into a generated _build_info.py, but does not consider or dismiss hatch-vcs's `write`/template capability, which already solves exactly this problem (writing arbitrary metadata files at build time) independently of its version-management feature. This is prior art the plan should address before committing to a custom hook.
findings.location: AC #3 / 'What gets recorded' section / 'A hatchling build hook (BuildHookInterface) captures the commit SHA at wheel/sdist build into a generated, git-ignored src/rebar/_build_info.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief note to the plan: 'hatch-vcs write feature considered — rejected because [reason, e.g. adds a new dev dependency / does not support absent-git graceful omit / produces a format incompatible with our _build_info.py contract].' If hatch-vcs does cover it, use it instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one auditor-facing validation criterion, e.g. a dogfooding AC such as: 'Given a signed attestation produced by this change, running `rebar show` displays a `rebar-version:` line that an auditor can cross-reference with the rebar release/commit log' — or a before/after comparison showing old manifests parse to `None` and new ones parse to the expected stamp.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism from the auditor's perspective is present in the acceptance criteria — all ACs are system-internal checks (line present, parser returns value, tests pass) with no before/after workflow comparison, dogfooding scenario, or operational metric showing an auditor can actually use the stamp.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC like: 'An operator running `rebar show` on a newly-signed attestation sees a `rebar-version:` line; running it on a pre-stamp attestation sees no such line (returns None from manifest_rebar_version()). This can be verified via the existing show integration test or a new one.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the rebar-version stamp is injected inside build_manifest unconditionally (covering refresh_attestation automatically) or passed as a call-site argument (requiring refresh_attestation to be listed as an edit target).
findings.finding: refresh_attestation in attest.py (line 446) also calls build_manifest directly but is not named as an edit target. If the rebar-version stamp is appended unconditionally inside build_manifest, it is automatically included — but if the stamp is added as an explicit call-site argument (like regver/refreshed_from), refresh_attestation is an unenumerated call site that needs updating.
findings.location: AC1 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:474
findings.suggested_fix: Inject the stamp unconditionally inside build_manifest (like the dep lines are), so refresh_attestation and any future callers inherit it without explicit updates.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all callers of `verify_signature` and specify whether the stamp is added to an existing dict/named-tuple (backward-compatible) or changes the return arity, so no existing caller breaks.
findings.finding: `verify_signature` is described as returning the new stamp, but the plan does not acknowledge existing callers of this function or specify how they should handle the changed return value/structure.
findings.location: AC: 'The stamp is surfaced in `show` (attestation block) and returned by `verify_signature`.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the stamp as an optional key in the existing return dict (e.g., `rebar_version: str | None`); document that callers not reading this key are unaffected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and test the `BuildHookInterface` hook's behavior under editable installs (`pip install -e .`): confirm that `_build_info.py` is either not generated for editable installs (hook skips) or that the live-git-first path reliably supersedes any baked value, so a developer never accidentally stamps a stale SHA.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a `BuildHookInterface` hatchling build hook to bake `_build_info.py` at wheel/sdist build time — new build infrastructure with zero prior hooks in this codebase. The plan explicitly rules out hatch-vcs ('do NOT switch to hatch-vcs') but doesn't clarify whether the hook mechanism conflicts with the 'version stays static' policy or whether the hook output is git-ignored correctly to avoid polluting editable installs.
findings.location: AC3 / Plan section 'What gets recorded', step 2 / Scope section 'Version stays static; do NOT switch to hatch-vcs — only add SHA capture'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
